title: The Stranger
author: Albert Camus
content: “I opened myself to the gentle indifference of the world.”
action: Record: Develop the capacity to operate with total detachment from external emotional validation; reality is neutral, not moral.

title: Waiting for Godot
author: Samuel Beckett
content: “They give birth astride of a grave, the light gleams an instant, then it's night once more.”
action: Record: Identify the 'Waiting for Godot' trap—the systemic paralysis caused by prioritizing an external trigger that will never arrive.

title: The Trial
author: Franz Kafka
content: “Logic may indeed be unshakable, but it cannot withstand a man who is determined to live.”
action: Record: When dealing with impenetrable bureaucratic systems, ignore the logic of the rules; focus solely on the agency of the individual actor.

title: No Exit
author: Jean-Paul Sartre
action: Record: Other entities in a system represent the primary source of friction and unpredictable variables; secure your own boundaries.

title: The Myth of Sisyphus
author: Albert Camus
action: Record: Derive satisfaction not from the completion of the task, but from the rigorous, repetitive application of effort against an unyielding system.
